title: Pegula Secures Second Title of 2026 with Charleston Open Victory
summary: Jessica Pegula, the top seed, successfully defended her Charleston Open title by defeating Yuliia Starodubtseva in straight sets, 6-2 6-2. This victory marks Pegula's second tournament win of the year, following her earlier success at the Dubai Tennis Championships. Throughout the tournament, Pegula faced challenging three-set matches to advance to the final, and in the championship match, she demonstrated resilience by saving seven out of eight break points. Her consistent performance this season has resulted in 24 wins, placing her ahead of world number one Aryna Sabalenka in the WTA win leaderboard for the year. Pegula expressed gratitude to the fans for their unwavering support during the demanding week. Meanwhile, Starodubtseva, despite struggling with 41 unforced errors in the final, achieved a career milestone by climbing 36 ranks to reach a new high of 53 in the world rankings, highlighting her breakthrough run in South Carolina.

background_read: The Charleston Open is a professional tennis tournament on the WTA tour, held in South Carolina. Players earn points for wins, affecting their world rankings. Break points are critical moments where a player can break the opponent's serve. The WTA leaderboard tracks seasonal performance, and unforced errors often indicate pressure or inexperience. Tennis matches can vary in length, with three-set matches being more physically demanding.
Article_Structure: Main Points: Pegula retained her Charleston Open title with a straight-sets win over Starodubtseva, marking her second 2026 victory and boosting her WTA win lead. Purpose: To report on Pegula's successful title defense and Starodubtseva's ranking improvement, highlighting tournament outcomes and player performances. Evidence Evaluation: The article provides specific scores, statistics like break points saved and unforced errors, and quotes from Pegula, offering reliable data from the match. Author Credibility: As a BBC Tennis report, it likely comes from professional sports journalists with access to official match details. Methodology: Information is gathered from live match coverage, player interviews, and official WTA statistics, ensuring accuracy and timeliness.

perspectives.description: Pegula's win demonstrates her elite skill and consistency in professional tennis.
